---
title: "BosphorusBytes"
sidebar: false
format:
  html:
    toc: false
  pdf:
    colorlinks: true
    papersize: a4
    fontsize: 11pt
    margin-left: 2cm
    margin-right: 2cm
    margin-top: 2cm
    margin-bottom: 2cm
---

# Istanbul Airbnb Analysis: How Short-Term Rentals Are Reshaping the City

---

## Team Members

| Name | Student ID |
|------|-----------|
| [Mevlüt Görgülü](https://github.com/) | 2210469027 |
| [Sıla Nur Güney](https://github.com/) | 2210469060 |
| [Aleyna Asiye Koç](https://github.com/) | 2210469076 |
| [Melike Ebrar Şahinbay](https://github.com/) | 2210469077 |

**Group:** BosphorusBytes &nbsp;|&nbsp; **Course:** EMU430 Data Analytics — 2024–2025 Fall &nbsp;|&nbsp; **GitHub:** [emu-hacettepe-analytics/BosphorusBytes](https://github.com/emu-hacettepe-analytics/emu430-fall2024-team-bosphorusbytes-3)

---

## Project Description

Istanbul is one of the world's most-visited cities, and the rise of Airbnb has fundamentally changed its short-term rental landscape. This project analyses the **Istanbul Airbnb dataset** from [Inside Airbnb](http://insideairbnb.com) to uncover how short-term rentals are distributed across the city, what drives listing prices, how the market evolved over time (including through COVID-19), and what kind of tourism pressure individual neighbourhoods face.

We combine **spatial mapping**, **regression modelling**, **time-series analysis**, **host profiling**, and **text analysis** to paint a comprehensive picture of Airbnb's footprint in Istanbul.

---

## Key Takeaways

- 🗺️ **Listings are heavily concentrated in tourist cores:** Beyoğlu, Şişli, and Fatih together account for over 45% of all Istanbul listings, while most outer districts have fewer than 100 listings each.

- 💰 **Location and room type are the strongest price predictors:** Entire-home listings in central districts cost 2–3× more than private rooms in peripheral areas; superhost status adds a further 10–15% premium.

- 📉 **COVID-19 caused a near-total booking collapse:** Monthly review counts — a reliable proxy for bookings — fell by ~85% between February and May 2020, with full recovery not achieved until mid-2022.

- 🏠 **A small group of professional hosts controls a disproportionate share of supply:** The top 5% of hosts by listing count control over 30% of total listings, raising concerns about Airbnb's impact on housing availability.

- 🔤 **Guest reviews highlight location above all else:** The words *location*, *clean*, and *walk* dominate reviews in central districts, while *quiet*, *local*, and *value* characterise peripheral ones.

---

*Navigate to the [Data](data.qmd) page to explore the dataset, or go straight to the [Analysis](analysis.qmd) for findings and visualisations.*
